description: In ImmutabilityCheckVisitor.kt:25-42, the check resolves symbols via tables.getLocalSymbol() which uses the package-level symbol table, not function-local FnSymbolTable. Local val variables inside functions may not be found, causing the mutable field to be null and the check to silently pass, allowing mutation of immutable local variables.
title: ImmutabilityCheckVisitor does not properly check local variables

close_reason: Already fixed - Sum.toString() uses correct branch: if (ids.isEmpty()) lhs|rhs else ids[lhs|rhs]
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Types3.kt:144-151

The non-Option branch of toString() contains a dead ?: branch:

  ids.toString() ?: "$lhs | $rhs"  // ids.toString() is NEVER null

ids is List<TypeId>, so ids.toString() always returns non-null. The ?: branch is unreachable. For anonymous sum types (empty ids), user sees '[]' in type error messages.

Impact: Error messages like 'Expected type is int but got []' instead of 'Expected type is int but got string | float'.

Fix: Replace the else branch:
  if (ids.isEmpty()) "$lhs | $rhs" else "$ids[$lhs | $rhs]"
title: BUG-10: Sum.toString() dead code produces unreadable error messages

description: In AtomReader.kt:32-34, toLong() and toFloat() are called without try-catch on parsed number text. Very large integer literals exceeding Long.MAX_VALUE crash with NumberFormatException. Very large float literals silently become Infinity. Should produce a proper compiler error message.
title: No validation for numeric literal overflow

description: In ChiParser.g4:109-112, logical AND/OR (lines 109-110) have HIGHER precedence than bitwise AND/OR (lines 111-112) because they appear earlier in the ANTLR rule. This is backwards compared to every mainstream language. Standard precedence should be: BIT_AND > BIT_OR > AND > OR. Expression like 'a & b && c' will be parsed incorrectly.
title: Bitwise vs logical operator precedence is reversed in grammar

description: In LuaEmitter.kt, Chi identifiers like end, local, nil, repeat, until, goto, do are valid Chi names (per ChiLexer.g4:86) but are reserved words in Lua. When emitted as local variables inside functions (line 242: 'local ${term.name}'), they produce Lua syntax errors. Top-level names use __P_.name which works, but function-local variables have no such protection.
title: Chi identifiers that are Lua reserved words produce invalid Lua code

description: In ParserVisitor.kt:90, negation (-x) is desugared to LongValue(0) - x. If x is a float, the left operand has type int (LongValue), which is semantically incorrect. Should use a dedicated ParseNegation node or match the zero literal type to the operand type.
title: Float negation desugars to integer zero subtraction

description: CheckNamesVisitor creates scopes for lambdas, for-loops, and named functions, but NOT for blocks. Variables declared inside if/else bodies leak into the enclosing scope for name checking purposes. Example: 'if true { val x = 5 }; x' - the reference to x should be an error but won't be caught.
title: CheckNamesVisitor does not create scopes for blocks

description: GitHub issue #20. Add special syntax for defining maps in Chi.
title: Add special syntax to define a map

description: In TypeReader.kt:135-139, TypeConstructorRef.equals only compares baseType, ignoring typeParameters. This means List[int] == List[string] evaluates to true. Same issue exists for FunctionTypeRef (lines 109-114) which ignores typeParameters in equality.
title: TypeConstructorRef.equals ignores typeParameters

description: GitHub issue #14. Add concise syntax for defining sets, e.g. {1, 2, 3}.
title: Add special syntax to define a set

description: The grammar allows traitDefinition as a top-level construct and TraitReader exists, but ProgramReader.read() never calls TraitReader.read(), ParseProgram has no field for traits, and ParserVisitor has no visitTraitDefinition. Writing a trait definition produces no error but has no effect. Additionally, the grammar only allows traitDefinition before the first newline.
title: Trait definitions are parsed but silently ignored

description: In Unification.kt:37-41, Recursive types are unfolded by replacing the recursive variable with the Recursive type itself. If both sides are Recursive or the unfolded form doesn't match, constraints are re-enqueued with no depth limit or seen-set, potentially looping forever.
title: Recursive.unfold() can cause infinite loop during unification

close_reason: Already fixed - Type.union() returns Type, no 'as Sum' cast
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Types3.kt:48-49

Sum.create flattens and deduplicates types. If all input types collapse to a single type (e.g. Type.union(null, int, int)), create returns a single Primitive, and the 'as Sum' cast throws ClassCastException.

Impact: Internal compiler crash if union is constructed with duplicate types.

Fix: Either guard union() to return Sum only when 2+ distinct types remain, or change the return type to Type and remove the 'as Sum' cast. Check each call site.
title: BUG-11: Type.union() crashes with ClassCastException on duplicate types

description: In InferenceContext.kt:83, when symbol.type is null, the code hits TODO() which throws NotImplementedError at runtime. Should produce a meaningful compiler error message instead of crashing.
title: TODO() crash in InferenceContext.listCurrentPackageFunctionsForType

close_reason: Already fixed - visitBlock propagates it.used = block.used
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/compiler/Usage.kt:69-73

In visitBlock, the last expression is unconditionally marked used=true, ignoring block.used:

  block.body.lastOrNull()?.let {
      it.used = true    // unconditional, ignores block.used
  }

Compare with the correct pattern in visitIfElse which propagates the parent's used flag.

Impact: Affects the Is expression check (Usage.kt:124-131): an 'is' check on a type variable inside a discarded block would incorrectly throw a compile error. Also affects LuaEmitter behavior for expressions that check the used flag.

Fix: Propagate the flag:
  it.used = block.used
title: BUG-12: Block.used flag not propagated — last expression always marked used

close_reason: Already fixed - Handle and EffectDefinition emission is implemented in LuaEmitter
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/lua/LuaEmitter.kt:156-199

The Lua code generation for EffectDefinition and Handle is entirely commented out. The 'when' in emitExpr falls through to:

  else -> TODO("Term $term not supported yet!")

The compiler front-end (parser, type checker) still accepts these constructs without error.

Impact: A Chi program using effect or handle compiles through all front-end phases, then crashes with kotlin.NotImplementedError during Lua emission. No helpful diagnostic.

Fix: Either re-implement the Lua emission for effects/handle, or reject these constructs early with a clear error message in Compiler.kt after AST conversion.
title: BUG-15: Effects/Handle compile but crash at Lua emission

close_reason: Already fixed - visitFunction uses function.copy(types = ...) preserving defaultArgs and typeParams
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/TypeVisitor.kt:16-17

VariableMapper base class reconstructs Function using only the types field, discarding typeParams and defaultArgs:

  override fun visitFunction(function: Function): Type =
      Function(function.types.map { it.accept(this) })

Function data class has three fields: types, typeParams, defaultArgs. VariableMapper is the base for FreshenAboveVisitor, used by PolyType.instantiate. Every time a polymorphic function type is instantiated, defaultArgs resets to 0 and typeParams are erased.

Impact: Polymorphic functions with default arguments lose their default arg count after instantiation, causing arity errors at call sites where default args are omitted.

Fix: Change to use copy():
  override fun visitFunction(function: Function): Type =
      function.copy(types = function.types.map { it.accept(this) })
title: BUG-05: VariableMapper.visitFunction drops defaultArgs and typeParams

description: In LuaEmitter.kt:532, bitwise operators &, |, <<, >> are emitted verbatim as infix operators. LuaJIT (Lua 5.1 syntax) does not support these - it requires bit.band(), bit.bor(), bit.lshift(), bit.rshift() library functions. Emitting them as infix operators produces Lua syntax errors at runtime.
title: Bitwise operators emit invalid Lua syntax

description: In LuaEmitter.kt:532, comparison operators for strings are not specially handled. Since Chi strings are Java String objects (userdata in Lua), these operators try to use __lt/__le metamethods which are not defined for Java userdata, causing 'attempt to compare two userdata values' runtime error.
title: String comparison operators <, <=, >, >= cause Lua runtime error

description: In ExprConversionVisitor.kt:35, currentInput() returns weaveInput!! which is null outside of a weave expression. Using _ (placeholder) in a non-weave context like foo(_) crashes the compiler with NullPointerException instead of producing a meaningful error.
title: Placeholder _ outside weave expression crashes compiler with NPE

description: In TypeReader.kt:97-98, TypeNameRef.equals only compares typeName, ignoring moduleName and packageName. This means mymod::mypkg::Foo equals othermod::otherpkg::Foo, which can cause silent type confusion between packages.
title: TypeNameRef.equals ignores moduleName and packageName

close_reason: Already fixed - visitSum passes typeParams = sum.typeParams to Sum.create
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/TypeVisitor.kt:24-29

Sum.create call in visitSum does not pass sum.typeParams, so type parameter names are lost during variable mapping (including during FreshenAboveVisitor instantiation). Same class of bug as BUG-05.

Impact: Parametric sum types (e.g. Option[T]) lose their type parameter names when instantiated, which can affect downstream type parameter resolution.

Fix: Pass typeParams to Sum.create:
  Sum.create(
      ids = sum.ids,
      lhs = sum.lhs.accept(this),
      rhs = sum.rhs.accept(this),
      typeParams = sum.typeParams
  )
title: BUG-13: VariableMapper.visitSum drops typeParams

description: In VariableReplacer.kt:16-17, visitSum calls Sum.create(sum.ids, replace(sum.lhs), replace(sum.rhs)) without passing typeParams. Sum.create defaults typeParams to emptyList(), so type parameters on Sum types are silently lost during variable replacement (unification). Compare with VariableMapper.visitSum in TypeVisitor.kt:24-30 which correctly passes typeParams = sum.typeParams.
title: VariableReplacer.visitSum silently drops typeParams during substitution

description: In LuaEmitter.kt:521-535, the == operator for strings correctly uses $leftVar:equals($rightVar) (Java equals for value comparison), but != is mapped to Lua ~= which compares userdata by reference. Two Java String objects with the same content would be considered unequal. Fix: add string-specific case for !=: (not $leftVar:equals($rightVar)).
title: String != operator compares by reference instead of value

description: In Types3.kt:153-155, Sum.removeType calls types.reduce on the result of listTypes(this) - type. If the Sum contains only the removed type, the collection is empty and reduce throws UnsupportedOperationException. Called from Typer.kt:269. Needs a guard for the empty case.
title: Sum.removeType crashes with UnsupportedOperationException on empty result

close_reason: Already fixed - debug = false in Constraint.kt
description:
File: src/main/kotlin/gh/marad/chi/core/types/Constraint.kt:5

  private const val debug = true

Every Constraint construction creates a RuntimeException() and captures its stack trace. This happens for every single constraint generated during type inference — hundreds or thousands per program.

Impact: Significant performance degradation during compilation. No functional bug, but noticeable slowdown.

Fix: Change to false, or remove the debug machinery, or gate behind a system property/env variable.
title: BUG-14: Debug stack trace capture always enabled in Constraint

description: In LuaEmitter.kt:493, emitBlock(term.thenBranch as Block) hard-casts thenBranch to Block. When when-expressions are desugared into nested IfElse chains, the thenBranch could be any Expression, not necessarily a Block. This throws ClassCastException at code generation time for single-expression when-case bodies.
title: emitIfElse unsafe cast: thenBranch as Block can throw ClassCastException

description:
GitHub issue #1. Support destructuring assignments:

For pairs:
  val (a, b) = Pair(1, 2)

For arrays:
  val (head, tail) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
  val (first, second, rest) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
  val (first, second, third, _) = arrayOf(1, 2, 3)
title: Value destructuring

description: test
title: test

description: In Types3.kt:187-212, Array.ids is a private mutableListOf(...) but NOT a constructor parameter, so Kotlin data class ignores it in equals/hashCode. Two Array instances with different ids are considered equal. Also getTypeIds() returns a reference to the mutable list, allowing external mutation. withAddedTypeId/withAddedTypeIds have subtle duplication bugs with ids.drop(1) after copy().
title: Array.ids is mutable and excluded from data class equals/hashCode

description: In Typer.kt:117, FieldAccess handler calls unify(constraints) on the full shared mutable constraint list mid-inference to resolve the receiver type. This can produce premature/incorrect solutions. Same pattern at NameDeclaration (line 146) and ForLoop (line 200). The solutions are not applied back, potentially causing inconsistencies.
title: Mid-inference unify() on shared mutable constraints may cause inconsistencies
